summary: High‑energy ’79 show captured on a solid soundboard tape, highlighted by a monster Scarlet > Fire and fluid half‑step Franklin’s.
review: The recording is a clean SBD that, despite the tracks being out of order, delivers clear instrument separation and a punchy mix. The concert bursts with mid‑’79 energy – the second set rides on a scorching Scarlet > Fire and inventive half‑step Franklin’s while still showing the occasional slip in timing on Saint of Circumstance and Sailor > Saint.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give 4‑5 stars, praising the performance and soundboard quality; only minor criticisms about song order and occasional sloppiness keep the rating just below top‑tier.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced mix praised as “fantastic” and “quite fine”; some reviewers note that the fileset is out of order for second set/encore but no major audio defects.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Energetic first set leads into a high‑octane second set; the band shows typical mid‑'79 looseness but maintains strong momentum, especially during the long Scarlet > Fire jam.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Garcia’s soloing on Scarlet > Fire and the half‑step Franklin’s is praised as focused, inventive and gorgeous; he’s also blamed for a timing slip in the Wharf Rat break.
band_member_comments.Phil: Phil’s bass is described as booming (“inside your skull”) and his intro to Fire was overridden by Jerry’s solo choices.
band_member_comments.Bob: Bob’s vocals stumble on Saint of Circumstance and he “loses it” a bit at the end of Good Lovin'.
